rationale: This example shows the oxidation of a simple primary alcohol (1-pentanol) to the corresponding aldehyde (pentanal) using the classic Pfitzner-Moffatt conditions: dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) as the oxidant, dicyclohexylcarbodiimide (DCC) as the activator, and pyridinium trifluoroacetate as the mild acid catalyst. This represents the core transformation of the reaction family on a simple, acyclic, and unambiguous substrate.
collision_avoidance_note: The substrate is a simple primary aliphatic alcohol to avoid overlap with reactions specific to benzylic, allylic, or complex cyclic systems. The reagents (DMSO, DCC, acid catalyst) are explicitly included to define the Pfitzner-Moffatt conditions, distinguishing it from other activated-DMSO oxidations like the Swern or Parikh-Doering oxidations, which use different activators (oxalyl chloride/TFAA or SO3-pyridine complex, respectively).
